# Chapter 9: The discipline of estimation  (~80 pp)

- 9.1 Order-of-magnitude reasoning (10)
- 9.2 Fermi problems (15)
- 9.3 Estimation before calculation: a habit (10)
- 9.4 Sanity-checking calculated results (10)
- 9.5 Estimation under deep uncertainty (10)
- 9.6 The estimation environment: the formal `\begin{estimation}` block introduced (8)
- 9.7 Failure section: estimates that were never sanity-checked (e.g., space-shuttle O-ring failure rate) (10)
- 9.8 Closing: estimation as a moral discipline (7)

**Archetype**: scaling (canonical home of order-of-magnitude reasoning), uncertainty.
**Project**: 50 Fermi problems. Solve all 50. Compare answers to truth where it can be looked up.
**Exercises**: 50 (the project).
